video_title: Knight of the Laughing Tree: how did Jon Snow's parents meet?
claim: The mystery Knight of the Laughing Tree who jousted at the Tourney at Harrenhal in defense of Howland Reed was Lyanna Stark in disguise, and the incident is likely where her secret romance with Prince Rhaegar Targaryen began, eventually leading (via R+L=J) to the birth of Jon Snow.
sub_claims: The Knight was Howland Reed himself, possibly winning with the old gods' magical aid in answer to his prayer.
The Knight was Brandon Stark, defending Howland out of his rash, gallant personality.
The Knight was Eddard (Ned) Stark, which would help explain his lifelong loyalty to Howland Reed.
The Knight was Benjen Stark, who armed Howland and was enthusiastic about avenging him.
The Knight was Lyanna Stark in disguise.
The Knight was actually Bran Stark, warging back into young Howland's body from the future using greensight and old-gods magic to win the jousts.

The Knight of the Laughing Tree incident is where Rhaegar Targaryen and Lyanna Stark's secret romance began, possibly culminating in a secret marriage on the Isle of Faces later revealed through Bran's visions.
asx_verdict: Probably Lyanna Stark; the Bran-time-travel theory is dismissed as 'kinda ridiculous' despite having some thematic resonance.
notes: Centers on one mystery (Knight of the Laughing Tree identity) but extends into how Rhaegar and Lyanna's romance began, feeding R+L=J; almost entirely sourced from Meera Reed's story to Bran in ASOS, with brief show (Bran/Hodor S6 parallel) and community-theory (Isle of Faces secret wedding) asides.

paraphrase: The story of the Knight of the Laughing Tree is told by Meera Reed to Bran Stark in Book 3; Meera never names the crannogman, but it's implied he's a young Howland Reed, who traveled from the Neck to the Isle of Faces and then to Harrenhal for the tournament.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera Reed's story of the Tourney at Harrenhal)

paraphrase: The tourney's attendees are given by title/heraldry rather than name: 'the king' Aerys Targaryen and his son 'the dragon prince' Rhaegar; 'the White Swords' (Kingsguard) welcoming a new brother (young Jaime Lannister); 'the storm lord' Robert Baratheon; 'the rose lord' Mace Tyrell; and Elia Martell, whom Rhaegar married instead of Cersei Lannister.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: Tywin Lannister did not attend the tourney, reportedly angry at Aerys both for taking Jaime into the Kingsguard and for refusing to marry Rhaegar to Cersei.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: Howland Reed was attacked by three young squires while walking across a field, mocked as a 'mud-man' and 'frog-eater,' and beaten and cursed until a 'she-wolf' (Lyanna Stark) drove the squires off with a blunted sword and took him to her tent to treat his injuries.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: At the Stark tent, Howland met Lyanna's brothers: 'the wild wolf' Brandon Stark (~19), 'the quiet wolf' Eddard Stark (~18), and 'the pup' Benjen Stark (~14); Lyanna was about fifteen and Howland about sixteen.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: That night at the feast Prince Rhaegar sang 'a song so sad it made' Lyanna cry — presented as the first hinted connection between Rhaegar and Lyanna.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)
spoken_quote: a song so sad it made

paraphrase: At the feast, Ashara Dayne ('a maid with laughing purple eyes') danced with a Kingsguard, Oberyn Martell, Jon Connington, and Ned Stark, after Brandon asked her on Ned's behalf because Ned was too shy to ask himself.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)
spoken_quote: a maid with laughing purple eyes

paraphrase: Rumors hold that Ned or Brandon got Ashara Dayne pregnant; a year later she had a stillborn child, her brother Arthur Dayne was killed by Ned and Howland, and Ashara committed suicide, so the truth may never be known.
book_hint: referenced as fan rumor, not directly sourced to a chapter

paraphrase: Later at the feast, Howland spotted the three squires who had attacked him; each served a knight, from House Haigh, House Blount, and House Frey respectively.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: The Starks suggested Howland win back his honor by beating the offending knights in the tournament, but Howland felt he wasn't good at riding and jousting and feared he'd just make a fool of himself.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: That night Howland went out by the lake, looked toward the Isle of Faces, and prayed to the old gods of the north.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: A mystery knight with name and face hidden, identified only by a weirwood tree with a laughing face painted on his shield, appeared at the tourney, defeated the Haigh, Blount, and Frey knights at jousting, and returned their horses and armor only after they punished their squires for attacking Howland — restoring Howland's honor to the crowd's cheers.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: King Aerys, paranoid, saw the Tree Knight as an enemy and sent his son Rhaegar to find out who he was, but 'all they ever found was [the Knight's] painted shield, hanging ... in a tree.'
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)
spoken_quote: all they ever found was [the Knight's] painted shield, hanging … in a tree

paraphrase: The Knight is described as 'short of stature' and clad in 'ill-fitting armor made up of bits and pieces' — details that could fit the small Howland if he'd had to scrounge armor together to compete.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: Meera's story suggests the old gods or the green men of the Isle of Faces heard Howland's prayer by the lake and 'gave strength to his arm' so he could win back his honor.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: ASX argues Game of Thrones isn't a fairytale world where the weak beat the strong through righteousness alone — citing Littlefinger's duel loss to Brandon Stark as proof pluck doesn't overcome skill — and that the old gods aren't shown as benevolent, prayer-answering deities but are more associated with blood sacrifice, making magical intervention for Howland inconsistent with the series' established rules of magic.
book_hint: referenced in-world memory of the Petyr Baelish–Brandon Stark duel, likely AGOT

paraphrase: Bran himself concludes within the story that the Knight was Howland, but ASX argues this is suspicious because ASOIAF mysteries typically present an obvious answer first that turns out to be wrong (e.g., Jon Snow believed to be Ned Stark's bastard but actually Lyanna's son; Jon Arryn believed poisoned by the Lannisters but actually poisoned by Lysa for Littlefinger) before revealing a more complex truth.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story); Jon Snow/Jon Arryn parallels drawn from across the series

paraphrase: When Meera tells the story, Jojen seems surprised that Bran hasn't already heard it from his father Ned, suggesting Ned or someone close to him may have been the mystery knight.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: Benjen, Ned, and Brandon were all trained to ride and fight, so unlike Howland they wouldn't have needed divine intervention to plausibly win a few jousts.
book_hint: general characterization of the Stark children

paraphrase: Brandon was described as 'hot-blooded' and rash, a 'gallant fool' — a personality that fits someone who would defend Howland's honor by jousting in disguise.
book_hint: characterization of Brandon Stark, drawn from multiple POV recollections

paraphrase: Brandon was 'tall,' which conflicts with the Knight's 'short of stature' description, a problem for identifying him as the mystery knight.
book_hint: characterization of Brandon Stark

paraphrase: It doesn't make sense for Brandon to have competed in disguise as a mystery knight, since he later jousts openly as himself in the same tournament.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story) plus general tourney-at-Harrenhal lore

paraphrase: Ned was younger and shorter than Brandon, so he might better fit the Knight's short stature.
book_hint: characterization of Ned Stark vs. Brandon

paraphrase: Ned riding for Howland in disguise could help explain how Howland became such a loyal, lifelong friend to Ned.
book_hint: general relationship between Ned Stark and Howland Reed across the series

paraphrase: Ned is described as 'solemn' and 'somber,' responsible and reserved, with 'a certain distaste for the ... pageantry of tourneys' — not a good match for the showy, theatrical persona of a mystery knight bearing a 'laughing' tree shield.
book_hint: characterization of Ned Stark, drawn from multiple POV recollections
spoken_quote: a certain distaste for the … pageantry of tourneys

paraphrase: Benjen was fourteen or younger at the time, fitting the Knight's short stature, and he was the one who offered Howland armor and a horse to compete, showing enthusiasm for someone avenging him.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: At fourteen, Benjen was very young, and there's no suggestion in the text that he was particularly skilled at riding, fighting, or jousting, making him an unlikely candidate.
book_hint: general characterization of young Benjen Stark

paraphrase: As a teenage girl, Lyanna could fit the Knight's short stature and 'ill-fitting armor.'
book_hint: characterization of Lyanna Stark plus the Knight's description in Meera's story

paraphrase: Lyanna had strong motivation to help Howland, since she was the one who initially rescued him from the squires.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: Lyanna is described as 'wild,' 'willful,' and 'boyish,' and compared to Arya Stark — fighting to help a friend fits that personality, as it would fit Arya's.
book_hint: characterization of Lyanna Stark, drawn from multiple POV recollections

paraphrase: Lyanna being the Knight would add a twist of the assumed-male mystery knight turning out to be a girl; Meera describes the Knight's voice 'booming' through the helm, which could reflect the helmet's echo disguising Lyanna's voice.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story)

paraphrase: It's questionable whether a fifteen-year-old girl would be strong enough to use a lance in a joust.

In an unreleased Winds of Winter preview chapter, another fifteen-year-old girl, Elia Sand, jousts so often she's nicknamed 'Lady Lance' — offered as precedent that a girl that age could plausibly joust.
book_hint: TWOW preview chapter (unreleased; not part of the 5-book corpus) — Elia Sand, 'Lady Lance'

paraphrase: Roose Bolton says that Lyanna 'was half a horse,' and in the same statement says 'A great jouster must be a great horseman' — directly linking riding skill to jousting ability and implying Lyanna could have done well in a tourney.
book_hint: ADWD, Roose Bolton dialogue (likely a Reek/Theon POV chapter)

paraphrase: Jaime Lannister thinks that jousting is three-quarters horsemanship, reinforcing that Lyanna's renowned riding ability would translate into jousting skill.
book_hint: Jaime POV chapter (book uncertain)

paraphrase: King Aerys specifically sent Rhaegar to find and unmask the Knight of the Laughing Tree; later in the same tournament Rhaegar publicly crowned Lyanna Queen of Love and Beauty, and later ran off with her, sparking Robert's Rebellion and eventually leading to the birth of Jon Snow — ASX suggests the Rhaegar-Lyanna relationship may have begun when Rhaegar was sent to find the Knight.
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Meera's story); tourney-at-Harrenhal lore across the series

paraphrase: ASX speculates Rhaegar may have found and identified Lyanna as the Knight but chose to keep her secret, impressed by her boldness, beginning their romance: 'maybe some words were whispered in a tent of a night,' 'Words or kisses, maybe more.'
book_hint: speculative extrapolation, not directly sourced to a chapter
spoken_quote: maybe some words were whispered in a tent of a night

paraphrase: Harrenhal (where Rhaegar later 'abducts' Lyanna) is close to the Isle of Faces; fan theories hold that Rhaegar and Lyanna secretly married on the Isle of Faces among the weirwoods, which would let psychic Bran later witness the wedding in a vision to confirm R+L=J.

A 'crazier' alternative theory some fans hold: Bran Stark, using future greensight, warged into past-Howland at the moment of his prayer, adopting the mystery-knight identity with a weirwood-painted shield and using old-gods magic to win the jousts and restore Howland's honor — paralleling Bran's Season 6 breaking of Hodor's mind by reaching into his past self to make him 'hold the door.'
book_hint: Season 6 'hold the door' Hodor storyline (show-only at the time); applied speculatively to the ASOS Knight of the Laughing Tree story

paraphrase: This Bran-warging theory fits some of the talk of magic in Meera's story and would fulfill Bran's wish, expressed after hearing the tale, to be a knight for a day: Bran thinks 'A day would be enough.'
book_hint: ASOS, Bran POV chapter (Bran's reaction after Meera's story)

paraphrase: ASX ultimately judges the Bran time-travel theory 'kinda ridiculous' despite its thematic resonance, and concludes it was probably Lyanna Stark who was the Knight of the Laughing Tree.
